title: Understand: Reconstruction: An Overview
description: Check your understanding of Reconstruction with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: America’s Second Founding: Slavery, the Civil War, Emancipation, and Reconstruction

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was true of the period of Reconstruction?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: African Americans voted in large numbers.
African Americans held office in all levels of government.
The national government acted to protect the rights of African Americans.
The national government refused to recognize civil rights for African Americans.
Some formerly Confederate states attempted to secede from the Union again.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Thirteenth, Fourteenth, and Fifteenth Amendments were ratified during a transformative period after the Civil War that was known as \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What laws denied African Americans the rights of citizenship after the Civil War?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**The Thirteenth, Fourteenth, and Fifteenth Amendments are known collectively as the \_\_\_\_.**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**What was the impact of the Thirteenth Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It abolished the institution of slavery throughout America.
It prevented former Confederates from voting in national elections.
It admitted the southern states back into the Union.
It extended the right to vote to all citizens of the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
In*Dred Scott v. Sandford* (1857), the Supreme Court held that African Americans could not be U.S. citizens. 

**Which amendment invalidated that decision?**

[[☃ radio 1]]]]

**What were the goals of the Civil Rights Act of 1866?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To extend citizenship to any person born in the United States
To end the institution of slavery across the United States
To protect the rights of all citizens, regardless of race
To readmit southern states to the Union after the Civil War
To limit voting rights of formerly enslaved people

assessment_items.question_content:
**Implemented in states across the South after the Civil War, Black Codes barred African Americans from which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which Reconstruction-era organization helped formerly enslaved African Americans find lost family members, learn to read, and negotiate labor contracts? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Why is Reconstruction referred to as America’s “Second Founding”? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It led to major transformations of the U.S. Constitution.
It caused the federal government to lose several powers.
It gave state governments no power over their own policies.
It led to the ratification of a new constitution of the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the Fourteenth Amendment say?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Everyone born or naturalized in the United States is a U.S. citizen.
Congress has the power to pass an income tax.
African American women have the right to vote.
The institution of slavery is banned at the federal level.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Fifteenth Amendment extended the right to vote to which group?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
